page: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is a primary feature of this instance, which bundles 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity supports a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps, and no GPUs are present.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4969.98 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 322.22 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic performance is exceptional, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 10% tier at 87,228.27. Database operations and Redis SET operations, which reached 89.14 million operations per second, also demonstrate strong performance, alongside static web serving throughput at 65.68 GB/sec. Single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier.

Given the high density of local NVMe SSD storage and substantial DDR4 memory, the i4i.24xlarge offers high resource density for I/O-intensive workloads. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for applications requiring massive local storage throughput without the overhead of network-attached storage. The server is optimized for large-scale transactional databases, NoSQL databases, Redis caching layers, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, with benchmark highlights including top-tier cryptographic execution and strong memory bandwidth of up to 4969.98 GB/sec for cached reads. It also achieves 89.14 million Redis SET operations per second and 65.68 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Lacking a GPU, this instance is designed for high-throughput, I/O-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data-intensive applications requiring massive local storage capacity and high network bandwidth of 56 Gbps.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform featuring ninety-six virtual cores, substantial memory, and twenty-two terabytes of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR4 memory
22,500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core and top-tier cryptographic benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and I/O-intensive workloads
